Japanese: さあ、お待たせしましたァッ！
English: Now! The moment you've all been waiting for!

Japanese: ‥‥いよいよ本年度、全日本 ヒーロー・オブ・ヒーロー‥‥
English: Who will be this year's Grand Prix Champion?

Japanese: グランプリの発表ですッ！
English: Who will be our "Hero of Heroes"!?

Japanese: 去年、惜しくも敗れた 忍者ナンジャがいます！
English: Will it be last year's runner- up, Jammin' Ninja!?

Japanese: サイパンからやってきた、 キャプテン・サイパン！
English: Or maybe Captain Saipan all the way from the lovely tropical island of Saipan!?

Japanese: 『正義学園４年２組』のみんなも せいぞろいだッ！
English: I see the students of a certain Starry School are raring to win!

Japanese: ナゾのエスニック・ヒーロー、 オニャンコポンも健在です！
English: And Global Hero Onyankopon doesn't want to go home without the prize!

Japanese: ‥‥勝利の女神がほほえむのは はたして、どのヒーローなのかッ！
English: We hope lady luck is with all our heroes tonight!

Japanese: それでは、発表しますッ！
English: And now!

Japanese: 第３回・ヒーロー・オブ・ヒーロー グランプリは‥‥
English: The winner of the third annual Hero of Heroes Grand Prix...

Japanese: ソレガシなりィィィィッ！
English: IS ME!!

Japanese: うおおおおおおッ！ 突然現れた、われらがヒーロー！
English: Whoooooooooa! The true hero of the night has appeared in our midst!

Japanese: 今年のグランプリこそ、 この戦士！
English: It looks like this year's Grand Prix goes to this fantastic warrior!

Japanese: 大江戸戦士‥‥ トノサマン・丙（へい）！
English: The Nickel Samurai!

Japanese: 忍者ナンジャ、今年も受賞を 逃しました‥‥ッ！
English: Too bad, Jammin' Ninja. Looks like the title eluded you again this year!

Japanese: ３月２０日　午後　７時４２分 ホテル・バンドー　すみれの間
English: March 20, 7:42 PM Gatewater Hotel Viola Hall

Japanese: やった！　やったよなるほどくん！ トノサマンが！　トノサマンがぁ。
English: Alright! Yes! Did you hear that, Nick!? Did you!? The Nickel Samurai! He did it!

Japanese: よかったねえ。 ‥‥いやまったく。
English: Yeah, he sure did... *sigh* I'm getting too old for this.

Japanese: いやあ。ボクもＯＢとして ハナが高いですよ。
English: Ah, I'm proud of the guy for doing the series justice.

Japanese: あ、あの。どうやらみなさんの ごヒイキの殿方が、
English: U-Um... So the person everyone was cheering for...

Japanese: 賞をおもらいになったのですね？
English: I guess he got the prize?

Japanese: そうだよ！
English: Yup!

Japanese: はみちゃん、知らないの？ 《トノサマン・丙（へい）！》。
English: You know who we're talking about, right Pearly? "The Nickel Samurai"!

Japanese: はい。わたくし、日曜日はもっぱら 『世界こども名作劇場』を‥‥。
English: No. Every Sunday, I only watch the Educational Channel's "Kids' Masterpiece Theatre".

Japanese: ダメだよ、はみちゃん。 ヤングはみんな、トノサマンなの！
English: OK, that's it! From now on, it's "The Nickel Samurai"! All the kids watch it!

Japanese: なるほどくんもやはり、 トノサマンがお好きなのですか？
English: Do you like "The Nickel Samurai" too, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: あー、ダメダメ。なるほどくん、 もうヤングじゃないから。
English: Nah, Nick's an old fart, so he's not allowed to watch it anymore.

Japanese: そうだな。ぼくは『世界こども名作 劇場』のほうが好きだけど。
English: That's right. But I do like "Kids' Masterpiece Theatre".

Japanese: うわあ。 お子さまだねー、なるほどくんは。
English: Hey, I didn't know you were so young at heart, Nick!

Japanese: まあ！　ダメです、なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick! You're a grown-up! You're not allowed to watch it anymore!

Japanese: いとしいヒトには、ムリヤリでも シュミを合わせる。
English: You're supposed to act your age and have interests that match.

Japanese: ‥‥大切なコトです。
English: ...It's very important.

Japanese: も、もう！ はみちゃん、そればっかり！
English: Aw, give it a rest, Pearly!

Japanese: 今日はみなさんを招待して、 大正解でした。
English: Looks like I made the right choice in inviting everyone here.

Japanese: よろこんでもらえたみたいで。
English: I'm glad you're all having a good time.

Japanese: もお、ユメみたいですッ！
English: Aaaaah... It's like a dream!

Japanese: でも、忍者ナンジャは キノドクでしたね。
English: Too bad for the Jammin' Ninja, though.

Japanese: 去年も、ヒメサマンに賞を 持って行かれたから、
English: Last year, he lost to The Pink Princess: Warrior of Little Olde Tokyo.

Japanese: 今年こそは、と 思っていたのですが‥‥。
English: I thought this might be his year...

Japanese: そうですねー。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: あ。でも、今日の忍者ナンジャ‥‥ ちょっと、ヘンだったなあ。
English: Oh, hey, did anyone else think that the Jammin' Ninja was a bit different today?

Japanese: ヘン、といいますと？
English: Different? What do you mean?

Japanese: うん。持ってなかったの。 ‥‥まっかなギター。
English: Um, well, he wasn't carrying his bright red guitar.

Japanese: お。そういえば。 ‥‥トレードマークなのに。
English: Hey, you're right! Strange he'd walk around without his signature guitar.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。ほっとくと いつまでも盛り上がっているぞ）
English: (...*sigh* I will never understand these people and their shows...)

Japanese: とにかく、荷星さん。今日は ありがとうございました。
English: Anyway, Mr. Powers, thank you very much for tonight.

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。成歩堂さんには ホント、カンシャしてますから。
English: A-Aw, it was nothing... I owe you one, so it's just my way of saying thanks.

Japanese: なるほどくん！　そろそろ ロビー行こうよ、ロビー！
English: Hey, Nick! Come on! It's time to get going to the lobby!

Japanese: 受賞記念のアトラクション・ショー があるんだよ！
English: There's a post-ceremony stage show that's supposed to start real soon.

Japanese: ショーのあとには、 記者会見もあるそうですから。
English: And then, I heard there's going to be a press conference after that.

Japanese: 記者会見？ ‥‥受賞のコメントですか？
English: A press conference? Is he going to make a speech about winning this year's prize?

Japanese: いえ。‥‥なんだか、告白が あるらしいです。トノサマンから。
English: Uh, well, not exactly... Something about the Nickel Samurai confessing something.

Japanese: コクハク‥‥？ また、たいそうなハナシですね。
English: Confessing...? Sounds pretty serious.

Japanese: もう！　なるほどくん！ ぐずぐずしない！
English: Argh! Nick! COME ON! You don't want us to be the last ones there, do you!?

Japanese: ダメですよ。 ぐずぐずしては。
English: Yeah, Mr. Nick! Do you!?

Japanese: （やれやれ。ショーが始まるまで、 まだ２０分もあるぞ‥‥）
English: (Why me...? The show doesn't even start for another 20 minutes...)

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。そろそろ時間ですね。 アトラクション・ショー。
English: Sounds like the post-ceremony show is about to start.

Japanese: わあ、たのしみだなあ！
English: Alright! I'm so pumped!

Japanese: 今日は出るかな？ “トノサマン・ザ・ご乱心”。
English: I wonder if he's going to show off his special move today? "Nickel Samurai Smelting!"

Japanese: ボクはどちらかというと、 記者会見のほうが気になります。
English: Actually, what I'm interested in most is the press conference.

Japanese: ショーのあとに、トノサマンが コクハクする、ってヤツですよね？
English: You mean the "big confession" the Nickel Samurai's going to make after the show?

Japanese: ニボサブさん、知らないんですか？
English: So what is it? Don't you know what it's about, Mr. Powers?

Japanese: は。いえ、ボクはもう トノサマンじゃありませんから。
English: Uh, well, I'm not the Steel Samurai anymore, so I don't have any idea.

Japanese: つまんないの。
English: Bah.

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: S-Sorry.

Japanese: あ、成歩堂さんたちも 聞きませんか？　その記者会見。
English: Ah, so I guess you are all going to the press conference then?

Japanese: 聞きたいッ！
English: Yeah, of course!

Japanese: じゃあこれ、あげますね。 記者会見の整理券です。
English: If that's the case, then here, take these tickets so you can get in.

Japanese: 《整理券》を法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Press Conference Ticket added to the Court Record.

Japanese: どうもすみません。‥‥じゃ、 とりあえずロビーに行こうか。
English: Thank you very much. Well, let's get going to the lobby.

Japanese: あ。こちらみたいですよ、 なるほどくん！
English: It looks like it's over this way, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: よーし。たたかうぞお！
English: OK! For great justice!

Japanese: それにしても、 さすが高級ホテルだよねー。
English: What can I say? This is a really high-class hotel!

Japanese: ホント、こんなおサラダ、 食べたことありません。
English: I've never eaten this kind of salad before.

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、ヤサイしか 食べないんだよな‥‥）
English: (Poor Pearls, having eaten only vegetables all her life. She's been missing out...)

Japanese: いいじゃない。残った お料理はあたしが食べるし。
English: Don't worry! Any leftovers belong in my happily awaiting stomach anyway!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは食いすぎだよ。
English: You're such a glutton, Maya.

Japanese: ヘタしたら、ボクより 食べてますよね、真宵さん。
English: Ah, I lose this one to Maya. You sure can eat.

Japanese: 食が細いと、大きく なれませんからね！
English: Well, a growing girl needs her nutrients!

Japanese: （大きくなるつもり だったのか‥‥）
English: (A growing girl? Exactly how big do you plan on getting...?)

Japanese: ああ、ホント、今日は ユメみたいだなあ。
English: If this is all a dream, I don't ever want to wake up!

Japanese: いろんなヒーローにスター、 カントクさんまで見られたし。
English: And all the directors and stars that were here to see the heroes, wow.

Japanese: ぼくはほとんど 知らなかったぞ。
English: I had no idea who most of them were.

Japanese: わたくしも、です。
English: Neither did I.

Japanese: ダメだなー、なるほどくん。 ニュースばっかり見てちゃ。
English: Nick, you've got to cut that "news only" habit out.

Japanese: 日曜日の朝８時は トノサマン・丙でキマリ！
English: Your new show is going to be "The Nickel Samurai!" Every Sunday morning at 8!

Japanese: そうだ！　来週から いっしょに見ようよ。
English: Oh, I know! We can watch it together starting next week!

Japanese: あたし、たたき起こして あげるから。
English: I'll come wake you up extra early to make sure, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥いいよ、気をつかって くれなくても‥‥。
English: Ah, I'm fine... You know, you really don't have to on my account...

Japanese: さっきまで、授賞式が 行われていた。
English: The awards ceremony was just held on that stage.

Japanese: ‥‥ゴージャスだったな、 さすがに。
English: It was really fabulous.

Japanese: ちょっと、あのサーカスを 思い出しました。
English: You just reminded me of the circus for a second.

Japanese: ま、ああいう演出はね。 おヤクソク、ってヤツだから。
English: Well, shows like that are guaranteed to be good, you know?

Japanese: それにしても、 ゴウカなホテルだ。
English: This sure is one luxurious hotel.

Japanese: 何かにとりつかれたかのように、 ゴテゴテかざりつけされている。
English: Almost to the point of gaudy, with how it blends together everything "fancy" imaginable.

Japanese: （そういえば去年、あのボーイさん から暑中見舞いがきてたっけ）
English: (Speaking of fancy, didn't that bellboy give me something like that last year?)

Japanese: あそこに 大げさなドアが見える。
English: There's a grand set of doors over there.

Japanese: あのドアをぬけると、 大げさなロビーがあるはずです。
English: And behind those doors is an equally grand lobby.

Japanese: 行ってみましょうか、 なるほどくん。
English: Shall we go and take a look, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: あ、待ってよ。この大げさな デザート、食べちゃうから。
English: Hey, wait up! This grand dessert is calling to me... It's saying, "Eat me now!"

Japanese: リッパなシャンデリアだよねー。
English: That chandelier is really something, isn't it?

Japanese: わたくし、宇宙船のたぐいかと 思いました。
English: I thought it was a kind of space ship...

Japanese: ね。ね。ウチの事務所にも ひとつ、ぶら下げようよ。
English: Hey, Nick. How about we get one of these for the office?

Japanese: あんなの天井からブラさげたら ぼくたちが入れないぞ、部屋に。
English: One of those hanging from our poor, weak ceiling? I don't think so.

Japanese: 今日は招待してくださって、 ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you very much for inviting us today.

Japanese: は。いや、きょ、恐縮です。
English: Aww, it was nothing really.

Japanese: みなさん、こういうところに来る 機会は、あまりなかろうかと。
English: Guys like us don't get to come to a place like this often, so I thought I'd invite you all.

Japanese: ね。ね。ニボサブさん！ 今は何をしてるんですか？
English: Hey, Mr. Powers! What have you been up to lately?

Japanese: はい。先月で、ヒメサマンが ブジ終了しまして、
English: Well, since "The Pink Princess" successfully wrapped up last month,

Japanese: 今はコドモ番組で、体操の お兄さんをやってます。
English: I've been on a kid's exercise show...

Japanese: ‥‥ウサギさんのマスクを かぶって。
English: ...while wearing a rabbit mask over my face...

Japanese: へええ。
English: Oh, I see.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。そのせつはホント、 お世話になりました。
English: I'm still really sorry about all the headaches I caused you that time, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: い、いえ。どういたしまして。
English: Ah, well. What's done is done, so let's forget about it.

Japanese: （この人は、荷星　三郎さん。 アクション俳優だ）
English: (This is Will Powers. He's an action star.)

Japanese: （《大江戸戦士　トノサマン》で 人気がバクハツした）
English: (His popularity exploded when he was the Steel Samurai.)

Japanese: （ぼくと真宵ちゃんが 初めて弁護したヒトでもある）
English: (And he was the first case Maya worked on with me.)

Japanese: カオに似合わず、子ども好きで ヤサシイ人なんだよね。
English: You can't tell by looking at you, but you're a really nice guy with a love of kids.

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: Ah, thanks.

Japanese: まさか映画になるとは思いません でしたよ。トノサマン。
English: I can't believe they're going to make a movie based on the Nickel Samurai.

Japanese: あたしは信じてたけどね。
English: I can't believe it either, but for a different reason!

Japanese: 大江戸戦士・トノサマン。 ネオ・エドシティを舞台に、
English: The Steel Samurai. An epic story of one hero in a desperate fight against

Japanese: 宿敵・怪人アクダイカーンと 死闘をくり広げるヒーローの物語。
English: his arch-enemy, the Evil Magistrate in the city of Neo Olde Tokyo.

Japanese: 去年から始まった新シリーズが、 《トノサマン・丙（へい）！》
English: And last year, they started a new series, "The Nickel Samurai".

Japanese: こちらもまた、子供たちに 大人気らしい。
English: The new series seems to be a hit with the kids too.

Japanese: トノサマンは、ボクにとっても 思い入れの強い番組だったので、
English: The Steel Samurai is a show I have a lot of strong feelings for,

Japanese: できれば、またボクが やりたかったんですケド‥‥。
English: so I hoped that maybe I'd get a chance to do something in this new one...

Japanese: しかたないですよ。
English: Yeah... It's too bad... It'd be great to see you on the show...

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟（おうとろうしんご） ‥‥今、人気急上昇中ですから。
English: With the new actor, Matt Engarde. He's super popular right now.

Japanese: オオトロ‥‥さん、ですか。
English: Mr. Un-gard...?

Japanese: （春美ちゃんは 知らないみたいだな）
English: (Looks like Pearls doesn't know who he is.)

Japanese: 今年は映画で対決だったんだよ。 トノサマンと忍者ナンジャ。
English: This year it's going to be the Nickel Samurai vs. the Jammin' Ninja at the box office!

Japanese: ‥‥にんじゃなんじゃ？
English: ...Jammin' Ninja...? Who's that again?

Japanese: ナニ？　忍者ナンジャって。
English: So who is this "Jammin' Ninja" again?

Japanese: やっぱり、ヒーローだよ。 ハデでまっかなギターがシンボル。
English: He's a hero, duh. His symbol is a bright red guitar he's always carrying.

Japanese: （ハデでまっかなギターをかかえて 忍者がつとまるのか‥‥？）
English: (A ninja who's always carrying around a bright red guitar...? How does that even work!?)

Japanese: ジマンのノドとギターを武器に、 室町のスターダムにのしあがるの。
English: With a scarf around his neck and a guitar in hand, he rises to stardom in an ancient time.

Japanese: ‥‥忍者が？　スターダム？
English: A ninja... Who becomes a star??

Japanese: うん。忍者が。スターダム。
English: Yeah! A ninja who becomes a star!

Japanese: ずーっとライバル関係なんですよ。
English: There's a strong rivalry between the two of them.

Japanese: 英都プロのトノサマンと、 光映プロの忍者ナンジャ。
English: Global Studios' Nickel Samurai and Worldwide Studios' Jammin' Ninja.

Japanese: ‥‥放映時間がかさなって いるものでして。
English: They even air at the same time.

Japanese: なんかね。 スゴく仲がワルいんだって。
English: You know what I heard? I heard those two don't get along at all.

Japanese: トノサマン・丙のオートロと、 忍者ナンジャ。
English: The Nickel Samurai's Engarde and the guy who plays the Jammin' Ninja, I mean.

Japanese: （オートロ‥‥王都楼　真悟か）
English: (The Nickel Samurai speaks French!? Oh, you mean Matt Engarde, the actor...)

Japanese: ‥‥ヒーローの世界も、 なんかドロドロしてるんですね。
English: I guess even the world of heroes isn't a sparkling, happy place.

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です‥‥。
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: いやー、そいつには お世話になりました。
English: Ah, I really owe you one.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんたちがいなかったら ボクはどうなっていたことか‥‥
English: If it wasn't for you guys, I don't know what would've happened.

Japanese: 本当に、大変なおシゴトだと 思いますよ。
English: What you did... It was a real tight spot I was in, and you fought so hard to get me out.

Japanese: ボク、何があっても 一生忘れませんから。
English: I don't care what happens. I'll never forget everything you've done for me.

Japanese: ヘンですよね。 いきなり決まったんですよ。
English: They called this so suddenly that something about it just seems off, you know?

Japanese: 記者会見、ですか？
English: You mean the press conference?

Japanese: ええ。“もし受賞したら コクハクしたいことがある”って。
English: Yeah. He said, "There's something I'd like to confess if I win."

Japanese: ファックスが入ったそうですよ。 ‥‥きのう。
English: I heard he faxed his request for a conference in to the office only yesterday.

Japanese: きのう‥‥。 （たしかに、いきなりだな）
English: Yesterday... (Yeah, that is kind of sudden.)

Japanese: オートロくんのマネージャーが 手配したらしいですけどね。
English: But I guess his manager set everything up somehow, so he was lucky.

Japanese: へえ‥‥。
English: Hmm... Nice manager.

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: S-Sorry.

Japanese: せっかく、そのようなものを つきつけていただいても、
English: Thanks for taking the time to take it out and show it to me,

Japanese: モウシワケありませんが、 心あたりがございません。
English: but I'm really sorry, I don't know a thing about it.

Japanese: やっぱり、真宵さんも 弁護士を目ざして‥‥？
English: So, you're going to study to become a lawyer too. Am I right or am I right?

Japanese: え。
English: Umm...

Japanese: タイヘンでしょうね。 おベンキョウのほう‥‥。
English: It must be real tough. There's all those thick books with hard words you have to read...

Japanese: い、いや、まあ‥‥。
English: Uh, um... Well...

Japanese: （荷星さん、知らないんだ。 ‥‥霊媒のこと）
English: (Oh, yeah. Mr. Powers doesn't know that Maya's a spirit medium...)

Japanese: この装束を見れば わかると思うケドな。
English: I thought he'd be able to tell by my clothes.

Japanese: ヘンなシュミの持ち主だと 思われてるんじゃないか？
English: Maybe he thinks that you've got some strange hobby on the side or something.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、ヤだなそれ。
English: ...Hey, wait a sec. What's that's supposed to mean!?

Japanese: そんなにコワいんですかねえ、 このカオ。
English: Hey, is my face really all that scary?

Japanese: 子どもたちはだれも 近よって来ないし‥‥
English: Kids won't come anywhere near me...

Japanese: ボクがアタマをかくと、 みなさんビクッとするし‥‥
English: But when I cover my face, they're all shocked because then they figure out who I am.

Japanese: うーん。あたしは そうは思わないけど。
English: Hmm... Well, I don't think you look scary.

Japanese: わたくしも、とても やさしいおカオだと思います。
English: Me either. I think your face makes you look like a very kind person!

Japanese: （荷星さん、 ナミダぐんじゃったぞ‥‥）
English: (Aww... Those two are making Mr. Powers tear up...)

Japanese: あの。真宵さんの 妹さんですか？
English: So are you Maya's little sister?

Japanese: いえ。ちがいます。
English: I'm sorry, but no.

Japanese: あ。じゃあ、成歩堂さんの 妹さん。
English: Oh, then you must be Mr. Wright's little sister!

Japanese: そうではありませんけど。
English: Again, I'm sorry, but...

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、じゃあ どなたの妹さんなんですか？
English: Then... Whose little sister are you?

Japanese: すみません。 ‥‥ひとりっ子なんです。
English: I'm sorry, but I'm an only child.

Japanese: あ、そうなんですか。 ‥‥きょ、恐縮です。
English: Oh, I see... S-Sorry.

Japanese: （おたがいに恐縮しあっている）
English: (Pearl looks so sad... She looks really sorry too...)

Japanese: ううう‥‥どうも そのようなかたは。
English: Umm... I'm honored you're asking me,

Japanese: 恐縮ですが。その。 ‥‥存じあげませんので。ええ。
English: But I have no idea who this person is. Umm... Sorry...

Japanese: そ、そこまで恐縮しなくても。
English: Ah, it's OK. You don't have to apologize so much.

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: S-Sorry.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ホテル・バンドー　すみれの間
English: March 20 Gatewater Hotel Viola Hall

Japanese: ‥‥特に変わったことはないね。
English: There's nothing really out of the ordinary here...

Japanese: おばさまは“事件があった”って おっしゃってましたよね？
English: Are you looking for the "some- thing" that the old lady was talking about?

Japanese: ここじゃないみたいだ。 他のところ、行ってみるか。
English: Doesn't look like it happened here. Well, we'd better go look somewhere else.

Japanese: よし！　じゃあ、今度は‥‥
English: Alright! Then, let's try...

Japanese: ‥‥シツレイですが。
English: Excuse me.

Japanese: きゃっ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: 綾里　真宵サマ‥‥ でございますか。
English: Are you by chance, Ms. Maya Fey?

Japanese: あ。はい‥‥ あたしですけど。
English: Um, yeah... That's me.

Japanese: お電話が入っておりますので、 フロントへおねがいします。
English: You have a phone call waiting for you at the front desk.

Japanese: でんわ？ ‥‥倉院の里のヒトかな。
English: A call? I wonder if it's someone from Kurain Village...

Japanese: どうしましたか、真宵さま？
English: What's wrong, Mystic Maya?

Japanese: ううん。なんでもないよ。 ‥‥先に行ってて。
English: Oh, nothing. I'll just go on ahead to check it out, OK?

Japanese: はい。わかりました。
English: OK.

Japanese: 綾里サマ。 こちらでございます。
English: Right this way, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: では、なるほどくん。 他の場所を見に行ってみましょう。
English: Let's go look somewhere else now, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: うん、そうだね。
English: Yeah, OK.

Japanese: ちょっと、どきどきします‥‥。
English: This is a little exciting... And a little scary...

Japanese: それにしても、 すごいごちそうでしたね。
English: It's a feast fit for a king!

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: わたくし、あのようなモノ、 初めていただきました。
English: It was the first time I ate that food.

Japanese: なんでしたか。たしか、 きゅうり、に似た名前の‥‥
English: What was it called again? I think it started with "key". Key... Wee?

Japanese: キウイ、ね。
English: That's right, a "kiwi".

Japanese: なんともいえない、 あまずっぱいくだものでしたね。
English: I don't know what to say. It's a sweet but sour fruit all in one.

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、ヤサイしか 食べないんだよな‥‥）
English: (Poor Pearls, having eaten only vegetables all her life. She's been missing out...)

Japanese: 料理はまだ かたづけられていない。
English: They haven't cleaned up all the food yet.

Japanese: パーティーは、終わってしまうと とてもサビシイ気がするんだ。
English: There's a sad feeling hanging in the air now that the party is over...

Japanese: さっきまで、授賞式が 行われていた。
English: The awards ceremony was just held on that stage.

Japanese: ‥‥ゴージャスだったな、 さすがに。
English: It was really fabulous.

Japanese: ちょっと、あのサーカスを 思い出しました。
English: You just reminded me of the circus for a second.

Japanese: みなさん、おゲンキでしょうか？
English: I wonder if everyone is alright.

Japanese: （タチミ・サーカスは最近、 公演を再開したらしい）
English: (I heard that Berry Big Circus just recently started holding performances again...)

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、ゲンキに やってると思うよ。
English: I'm sure they're all fine, Pearls.

Japanese: 大げさなロビーに通じる、 大げさなドアが見える。
English: There's a grand set of doors over there.

Japanese: ボーイにつれられた真宵ちゃんが 消えたドアだ。
English: It's the doors Maya followed the bellboy out of.

Japanese: しゃんでりあ、というもの らしいですね。
English: So, that's called a "shan-deh leer", right?

Japanese: ‥‥そうらしいね。
English: Yup.

Japanese: あれは、なんのために あるのですか？
English: What is that for? What does it do?

Japanese: そ、それはまあやっぱり、 部屋を明るくするためだね。
English: Well, it's for lighting up a room, Pearls.

Japanese: わたくし、見とれてしまって クビがいたいです。
English: Um, Mr. Nick? Can I look down now? My neck hurts...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 控え室前　ろうか
English: March 20 Gatewater Hotel Hallway

Japanese: うわ。なんだここ。 ヘンなトコにまよいこんだな。
English: Wow, what is with this place? Looks like I've stumbled into Oz or something.

Japanese: ‥‥奥に、トイレのドアが 見えるぞ。
English: Way in the back, there's a sign for the bathroom.

Japanese: ショーを見に行く前に、 寄っておこうかな‥‥？
English: I bet we have a little time to look around before the show starts...

Japanese: 楽屋の前に花があるのは ふつうだけど、
English: I can understand flowers in front of the dressing rooms,

Japanese: なんでこんなところに クマのぬいぐるみが‥‥？
English: but what are stuffed bears doing here...?

Japanese: もしかして、クマが大好きな アクション・スターなのか‥‥？
English: Could it be that there's an action star who has a soft spot for teddy bears...?

Japanese: ‥‥まさか、ね。
English: ...Nah, can't be.

Japanese: “王都楼さん江 英都プロダクション一同”‥‥
English: "To Mr. Engarde From the Global Studio Staff"

Japanese: 弁護士にも花輪が あればいいのにな。
English: Ah, it'd be nice if lawyers got flowers too. Something like,

Japanese: “成歩堂さん江 依頼人一同”‥‥とか。
English: "To Mr. Wright From All Your Grateful Clients".

Japanese: ドアに張り紙がある。 “王都楼　真悟様　控え室”
English: There's a piece of paper taped to the door that says, "Matt Engarde's Room".

Japanese: “オウトロウ　シンゴ”。 ‥‥聞いたことのある名前だ。
English: "Matt Engarde"... I've heard that name before.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんがウワゴトのように 連呼していたような‥‥。
English: Oh, yeah. Maya's always yammering about him like the obsessed fan she is...

Japanese: トイレなら、それぞれの 部屋にあるんだけど、
English: There are toilets in each room in this hotel,

Japanese: このホテルでは、いろいろな イベントが開かれるので、
English: but since all sorts of events are held here,

Japanese: 泊まっている客以外の人たちが 使うのだろう。
English: there are bathrooms for people who aren't staying here to use as well.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくもそのひとりだけど。
English: Perfect for people like me, who can't afford a stay here in the first place...

Japanese: 向かいの花輪にくらべて、 さらにゴージャスだ。
English: Compared to the flowers on the other side of the hall, these are much more gorgeous.

Japanese: 事務所、レコード会社、 ファンクラブ、兄弟一同‥‥
English: Let's see... Record companies, fan clubs, company workers, family...

Japanese: 持って帰るのが タイヘンそうだな。
English: Carrying all these flowers home would be hard, I think...

Japanese: “藤見野　イサオ様　控え室” 張り紙にはそう書かれている。
English: There's a piece of paper taped to the door that says, "Juan Corrida's Room".

Japanese: “フジミノ　イサオ”。 ‥‥たしか、スターの名前だ。
English: "Juan Corrida"... His name just sounds like a star's name.

Japanese: 聞いたことはあるけど、 よく知らないな。
English: I've heard it before, but I don't know anything about him at all.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 控え室前　ろうか
English: March 20 Gatewater Hotel Hallway

Japanese: だぁかぁらぁ‥‥、
English: I. TOLD. YOU.

Japanese: ウチは社会のマドなんや！ 知るケンリがあるんやッ！
English: I get people the info they want! Which means that I've got a right to know!

Japanese: ダメッス！　まだ捜査中ッス！ なんぴとたりとも‥‥
English: Nope! Don't care who you are, pal, we're still investigating so you can't go in!

Japanese: なァんやそのタイド！
English: What's yer problem!?

Japanese: 明日のチョーカン、 楽しみにしときィ！
English: Just ya wait! It'll be all over the morning paper!

Japanese: “ヒゲ刑事、スキャンダル発覚” ‥‥の見出し、オドらしたるワ！
English: "Scruffy Detective's Secret Scandal Revealed!" You'll see! I'll git ya back!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、すごいことに なっていますね。
English: Those two sound pretty serious...

Japanese: （あのイセイのいい声、 やっぱり‥‥）
English: (That southern accent can only mean...)

Japanese: あ。ナルホドー！
English: Hey, Wright!

Japanese: どうも‥‥。ナツミさん。
English: H-Hey... Lotta...

Japanese: アンタも言うたってや！ ウチには、取材のケンリが‥‥
English: Come on, do a gal a favor and tell this cop I'm just doin' my job and I've got rights...

Japanese: あ。アンタ！
English: Ah, YOU!

Japanese: どうも‥‥。イトノコ刑事さん。
English: Ah... Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: 言ってやってほしいッス！ 一般人は立入禁止ッス！
English: Hey pal, help a guy out! Tell her that only the police are allowed here!

Japanese: コトは殺人ッスよ！ ここはワレワレにまかせて‥‥
English: This is the scene of a murder, so she should leave this to us pros...

Japanese: さ‥‥さつじん！ A...
English: A murder!?

Japanese: しまった！　口がスベったッス！
English: Aw, shoot! Me and my big mouth!

Japanese: ホレ、見てみィ！ なーにが“胃のもたれ”やッ！
English: See! I knew it! My gut instinct told me so!

Japanese: もお、言いふらしたるでえ。 コロシやでー、ってなァ！
English: I always trust my gut! A murder, it said! And that's what it is! A genuine murder!

Japanese: あ、ちょっと‥‥
English: H-Hey, wait...

Japanese: コロシやでー！ ビッグスターがやられたでー！
English: Yeehaw, a murder! Of a big star, no less!

Japanese: うううう‥‥ッス。
English: Oh, man... I'm in trouble now...

Japanese: あの、刑事さん。 ‥‥サツジン、って‥‥？
English: So... Detective Gumshoe... A murder...?

Japanese: いや。その ‥‥マイったッスね。
English: Ah, no, that's not it. I got my facts mixed up for a second there, pal.

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥なるほどくん？
English: U-Um... Mr. Nick?

Japanese: まさか‥‥亡くなったかた、 トノサマンさんではないですよね？
English: Is the dead person the Nickel Samurai...?

Japanese: どうして？
English: Huh? Why do you ask?

Japanese: 真宵さまがおうえんされて いましたから。
English: Well, Mystic Maya was rooting for him, so...

Japanese: 殺害されたのは、 トノサマンじゃないッス。
English: It wasn't the Nickel Samurai that got bumped.

Japanese: ‥‥例によってトノサマンは、 やっつけた側ッス。
English: Actually, the Nickel Samurai is the one under suspicion of doing the bumping off...

Japanese: え。
English: What?

Japanese: やられたのは、忍者ナンジャ とかいうヒーローッス。
English: The guy that died was this hero named the Jammin' Ninja, pal.

Japanese: 忍者‥‥ナンジャ‥‥
English: The Jammin' Ninja...

Japanese: あの、コゲたワタあめのような アタマをした女のかたですけど‥‥
English: Um, that woman with the big puffy hair that looks like cotton candy...

Japanese: ああ、ナツミさん。
English: You mean Lotta?

Japanese: あのかた、あのときもいましたね。
English: That woman... She was there that time too, right?

Japanese: あのとき？
English: That time?

Japanese: ‥‥真宵さまが霊媒をなさって‥‥
English: ...When Mystic Maya did that channeling...

Japanese: ああ、この前の事件か‥‥。 彼女はね。ジャーナリストだから。
English: Oh, that time... Well, Lotta's a journalist, so that's why she was there.

Japanese: じゃーなりすと‥‥
English: Jer-nal-ist...

Japanese: なんか彼女、事件が起こる前から ずっと見張っていたらしいッス。
English: It looks like she was hanging around here before the murder happened.

Japanese: 見張っていた‥‥？
English: "Hanging around"...?

Japanese: 現場‥‥忍者ナンジャの 控え室前に、かくれていたッス。
English: Yeah, hiding in wait in front of the Jammin' Ninja's door, pal.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか？
English: B-But why would she...?

Japanese: 教えてくれないッス。 “スクープのためや”って‥‥
English: She wouldn't tell me, pal. Just said something about "Gettin' my big scoop"...

Japanese: （スクープ‥‥ いったい、なんの‥‥？）
English: (Scoop? What sort of news could she be after...?)

Japanese: 被害者ですけど‥‥ 忍者ナンジャ、ですか？
English: So the victim was the Jammin' Ninja?

Japanese: なんじゃさん‥‥
English: Mr. Jammin' Ninja...?

Japanese: 《トノサマン・丙！》と人気を争う ヒーロー番組ッス。
English: He was on a really popular rival TV show to "The Nickel Samurai".

Japanese: はああ。
English: Oh...

Japanese: 亡くなったのは藤見野（ふじみの） イサオ。アクション・スターッス。
English: The victim was the action star, Juan Corrida.

Japanese: アツくるしい芸風で大人気。 自分でも知ってるッス！
English: He got a huge push and rode the express train to stardom. I mean, even I know who he is!

Japanese: （ぼくもカオぐらいは 知ってるな、藤見野　イサオ）
English: (Yeah, even I recognize his face...)

Japanese: ま、今は王都楼　真悟の 人気に押されぎみッスけど。
English: But I heard lately that Matt Engarde's been taking the wind out of his sail.

Japanese: アクション界の人気は今、 この２人がうばい合ってるッス。
English: I'm telling you, pal, as far as who's popular, those two are hogging all the limelight.

Japanese: （荷星さんの入りこむ スキ間はなし、か‥‥）
English: (I guess there's no space for Mr. Powers at all, huh... Poor guy...)

Japanese: オートロさん、というのは‥‥ たしか《とのさまん・ほい》の‥‥
English: Mr. Engarde... Um... That's the Nickel Samurai, right...?

Japanese: そッス。いや、ちがうッス。 《トノサマン・ヘイ！》ッス。
English: Yup. I mean, no! You've got to say it with more umph! "The Nickel Samurai!"

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、藤見野　イサオが 亡くなったわけですから、
English: ...Anyway, so now that Juan Corrida's gone,

Japanese: 人気はすべて、王都楼さんが ひとりじめ‥‥ってコトですね？
English: that means Mr. Engarde has the whole stage to himself, wouldn't you say?

Japanese: ところが。 ‥‥そうはいかないッス。
English: I wouldn't bet on it, pal. We can't have that happen, you know?

Japanese: “そうはいかない”？ （‥‥どうしてだろ‥‥）
English: "Can't have that happen"? (What's that supposed to mean, I wonder...)

Japanese: ど、どうして王都楼さんが タイホされたんですか？
English: Wh-Why was Mr. Engarde arrested?

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンッスが。 それはヒミツッス。
English: Sorry, pal, but that's not something I can tell you.

Japanese: 捜査もまだ、始まったばかり。 情報は流せないッス。
English: We just started the investigation, so we don't want any leaks.

Japanese: （トノサマン‥‥ また、つかまっちまったのか）
English: (Looks like yet another "Steel Samurai" hero is in hot water...)

Japanese: あの、なるほどくん。 真宵さまがこれを知ったら‥‥
English: Um... Mr. Nick... If Mystic Maya knew about this...

Japanese: うん。きっと引き受ける コトになるだろうな‥‥弁護。
English: Yeah, I know. She'd make me take this case... I know...

Japanese: これ、見てください。
English: Would you take a look at this for me?

Japanese: ‥‥同じコト言わせないで ほしいッス。
English: Listen, pal...

Japanese: 証拠品に関する情報は、 特に流せないッス。
English: I'm not leaking any info about the evidence to you this time.

Japanese: これ以上、自分の給料に ナニかあったら‥‥
English: If I do, my salary's really gonna get it...

Japanese: ソーメンも食べられなく なるッス！
English: And then I won't even be able to get those instant noodles down at the discount market!

Japanese: ‥‥トノサマン・丙！ 今夜、グランプリをとったッス。
English: The Nickel Samurai! He really took the Grand Prix tonight!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、大ファンですよ。
English: Maya's a big fan of his.

Japanese: そッスか。 ‥‥それは、おキノドクッス。
English: Really? ...Oh, I'm sorry then.

Japanese: “おきのどく”‥‥？
English: Why are you sorry?

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟は先ほど、 緊急逮捕されたッス。
English: Matt Engarde was just arrested, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥藤見野　イサオ殺害容疑で。
English: ...On suspicion of murdering Juan Corrida.

Japanese: えええッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: 自分はあまり 活劇にはくわしくないッスが‥‥
English: I'm not a real fan of action shows or anything...

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオと王都楼　真悟 ぐらいは知ってるッス。
English: But I know who Juan Corrida and Matt Engarde are.

Japanese: この２人、いつでも名前が ならんでいますよね。
English: You can't talk about one without talking about the other, I guess.

Japanese: なにぶん、デビューも 近かったし‥‥
English: They even debuted around the same time.

Japanese: おたがい、ものスゴい ライバル意識を持ってるッスから。
English: So they have this real fiery rivalry with each other.

Japanese: まるで、自分とアンタみたいに！
English: Kinda like you and me, pal!

Japanese: （イトノコさん‥‥ぼくに ライバル意識を持ってたんだ）
English: (I never knew he thought of me as a rival...)

Japanese: 最近よく見かけるカオ、 第３位ッス。
English: That girl is number three on my "People I've Seen A Lot Of Lately" list.

Japanese: そうなんですか。 ‥‥あの、第１位は？
English: Oh? Then who's number one...?

Japanese: 聞くまでもなく、 アンタッス。
English: As if you need to ask. It's YOU, pal!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。 ちなみに、２位は？
English: A-Ah... So who's number two?

Japanese: 綾里　真宵ッス。 ‥‥今はいないみたいッスけど。
English: Maya Fey. ...But it looks like she's not around this time.

Japanese: きっと、よいお友だちなのですね。
English: It must be nice to be such good friends with each other, isn't it Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （きっと、それはちがうと思う）
English: (Somehow, I don't think that's the real meaning of Detective Gumshoe's list...)

Japanese: ちょっぴり ヤセた気がするッス。
English: I look good! I think I've lost some weight, pal!

Japanese: 気のせいですよ。
English: It's only wishful thinking.

Japanese: 気のせいッスか。
English: Wishful thinking, huh?

Japanese: やっぱり ヤセた気がするッス。
English: In that case, I wishfully think I've lost some weight!

Japanese: （だから、気のせいだって！）
English: (I wishfully think he might face the facts one day...)

Japanese: 最近、ソーメンしか 食べてないッスからねえ‥‥。
English: Lately, I've been on the instant noodles only diet, pal!

Japanese: ホント、この前の事件では ビックリしたッス。
English: I was really shocked by what happened to her in that one case.

Japanese: まさかあの子に、あんなスゴい 特技があったとは！
English: I didn't know she had such an awesome power!

Japanese: トクギ‥‥
English: Awesome power...?

Japanese: 自分も、ナニか 始めたくなって‥‥、
English: I thought maybe I should do some self-improvement and take up a hobby.

Japanese: 最近、あさがおのタネを まいてみたッス。
English: So I went out and bought some Morning Glory seeds and planted them, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥じょうぶに育つと いいですね。
English: ...That's a good way to improve yourself.

Japanese: はっはっはっ。 いや、マッタク。
English: Ho ho ho. Well, it's nothing really...

Japanese: この人のコト、知りませんか？
English: Do you know this person?

Japanese: 刑事はヒトと出会い、 そして別れをくり返すッス。
English: You meet a lot of people as a detective, pal.

Japanese: キオクを消すのもまた、 ワレワレのダイジなシゴトッス。
English: So we make extra sure to not forget people because that's our job.

Japanese: ‥‥というワケで その人物、見おぼえがないッス。
English: ...Now, having said that, I don't remember ever seeing this person before.

Japanese: つまり、刑事さんは忘れっぽい、 ということですね？
English: Which means that you're kind of forgetful, aren't you Mr. Detective?

Japanese: ‥‥そういうんじゃ ないッスけど‥‥。
English: That's not what it means... Not exactly anyway...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ホテル・バンドー　ロビー
English: March 20 Gatewater Hotel Hotel Lobby

Japanese: うーん。ゴウカなホテルだけあって ロビーも大げさだなあ。
English: Hmm... Only a really gaudy hotel would have such a large, gaudy lobby to match.

Japanese: あ。あそこでやるのですね。 アトラクション・ショー。
English: Ah, I think they're going to have the post-ceremony show over there.

Japanese: ステージはこぢんまりしてるね。
English: They're using a compact stage, I see.

Japanese: うう‥‥ウデが鳴るなあ。
English: Ooh... I'm all ready to use my special "Samurai Power".

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、たたかう気 マンマンだな‥‥）
English: (Maya looks like she's ready to start a fight...)

Japanese: ロビーにお集まりのみなさまに お知らせします。
English: Your attention please, your attention please.

Japanese: トノサマン・丙！　受賞記念 アトラクション・ショーは、
English: The Nickel Samurai's Post- Ceremony Stage Show

Japanese: つごうにより 中止させていただきます。
English: will not be held tonight due to unforeseen circumstances.

Japanese: うええええ。どうしてよッ！
English: WHAAAT!? WHYYYYY!?

Japanese: いてて。つねるなって。
English: Oww! You didn't have to pinch me! Pinch yourself if you don't believe it!

Japanese: みなさまに、ご協力を おねがいいたします。
English: We are asking for everyone's cooperation at this time.

Japanese: その場からはなれないように してください。
English: So please stay where you are.

Japanese: ‥‥これは、警察からの要請です。
English: ...This is a special request from the police.

Japanese: け‥‥ケーサツ、 って言ったよな？　今。
English: P-Police? Did they just say the police?

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと 見てきましょうか？　ボク。
English: D-Do you want me to go check out what's going on?

Japanese: あ。それなら、ぼくも‥‥
English: Um, wait, I'll come with you...

Japanese: 待ちなッ！
English: Freeze!

Japanese: アンタたちッ！ 放送が聞こえなかったのかい！
English: You two! Didn't you hear the announcement just now!?

Japanese: “動くな”って 言われたばっかりじゃないのサ！
English: It just finished telling you not to move!

Japanese: こ、この声。 ‥‥どこかで‥‥
English: Th-That voice... I've heard that voice somewhere before...

Japanese: まったくアレだョ。今の若い子は、 ヒトの話を聞かないネ。
English: Honestly, youth these days can't be bothered to listen to other people when they talk!

Japanese: この前もそうサ。コワれかけた橋の そばに、『この橋わたるべからず』
English: Just the other day it was the same thing! There was a small footbridge with a sign next to

Japanese: って書いてあったワケ。それなのに ちっこいボウズアタマのコゾウがサ
English: it that said, "Beware, Bridge Out!" And along comes a snot- nosed little punk kid right up

Japanese: わたろうとしてんのョ。オバチャン シンセツに“アブナイョ”って言っ
English: to the bridge. I tried to tell the boy the bridge was out and it was dangerous, but would he

Japanese: てるのに聞きゃしないんだからネ。 “ハシじゃなくて真ん中をわたれば
English: listen!? No, of course not! He said he'd be careful and only walk on the "in" part of the

Japanese: いいんです”とか言いやがるワケ。 ジョーダンじゃないっつーのホント
English: bridge since the "out" part is what was dangerous! I am not kidding you here! The kid said

Japanese: ナマイキなコト言ってんじゃないョ オバチャンたたき落としてやったネ
English: that and really meant it! WELL! I really let him have it then, and knocked him clear

Japanese: まったくザマァないやネいやホント
English: off of that bridge! Honestly, kids these days don't know right from wrong, I tell you!

Japanese: こ、このマシンガントーク‥‥
English: This non-stop chatter...

Japanese: も、もしかして ‥‥オバチャンですか？
English: I-It can't be... ...Ms. Oldbag?

Japanese: なんだいアンタたち。 オバチャンを知ってるのかい！
English: What is it, you young whipper- snappers!? Do I know you!?

Japanese: ‥‥って、アンタ！ ニボシじゃないのサ！
English: ...Wait. YOU! You're Powers, aren't you!?

Japanese: は、はあ。そのせつは‥‥
English: Y-Yeah. Um, about what happened back then...

Japanese: アンタ、今年はノミネートも されてないんじゃないのかい？
English: You didn't get nominated last year either, did you?

Japanese: たしかアレだ。コドモ相手の番組で いいヒトぶって体操してるんだろ？
English: Oh, that's right. You're doing that children's exercise program, trying to play nice.

Japanese: は、はあ。 おかげさまで‥‥
English: Ah, yeah, that's me. Thankfully I still have a job...

Japanese: いやいや笑わせるねェ。そのツラ ぶらさげて“おニイさん”とはねェ
English: I love that show, and you're a hoot! You're the "big brother" character, right?

Japanese: ああ、マスクしてるからカオは見え ないんだっけ。しかもウサギのお面
English: Yes, even with your face covered by a mask, I know. You ended up with a rabbit face.

Japanese: ケッサクだネまったく。でもサ。 そうでもしなきゃテレビがホレ、
English: What a work of art, but that's how it is, you know? I mean, if you didn't wear the mask,

Japanese: ぶっコワれちまうしネ。ホント、 アンタのまわりで体操してるコドモ
English: who knows how many TVs you'd break? Really, and shame on those kids exercising around

Japanese: たちのほうこそいいツラのカワだっ ての。マッタクこれがサ。
English: you. They're getting what they deserve. This is why I thought you shouldn't be anywhere...

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あの。 こんなところでナニを？
English: Um... What are you doing here?

Japanese: 見りゃわかるでショ。 警備員ョ。
English: Look at my uniform and tell me you can't tell I'm a member of security!

Japanese: でも、そのカッコ‥‥
English: But that outfit...

Japanese: うるさい子だね。撃つよ！
English: Annoying, noisy brats get the blaster!

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: ‥‥ね。ね。なるほどくん。
English: ...Hey, Nick.

Japanese: な、なんだよ、 そのキケンなほほえみは。
English: Wh-What is it? I don't like that devilish smile playing on your lips...

Japanese: ちょっとホラ、トイレ行くフリして 探検してこようよ！
English: Let's make like we're going to the bathroom, and check things out!

Japanese: だ、ダメだよ。“動くな” って言われてるだろ？
English: N-No way. The police told us not go anywhere, remember?

Japanese: えー！　つまんないよー。
English: Huh!? How B-O-R-I-N-G!

Japanese: ロマンのないオトコなんて モテないんだからね！
English: You're such a boring guy! You've got no motivation! No spirit!

Japanese: あ。なに？　なんですか？　また、 真宵さまを困らせてるんですか？
English: Huh? What? What's going on? Are you giving Mystic Maya trouble again, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （うう。メンドウな子が 出てきちゃったよ‥‥）
English: (Not you too, Pearls... Please don't stick your little nose into this one...)

Japanese: 聞いて聞いて、はみちゃん。 なるほどくんったらさあ。
English: So listen to this, Pearly. This one time at lawyer camp, Nick...

Japanese: わわ、わかったよ。 ちょっと見てこよう。
English: O-OK! I get it! Let's go take a look.

Japanese: わあい！ さすが、なるほどくん。
English: Yay! I knew you couldn't say no to me, Nick!

Japanese: そうですとも。
English: That's right.

Japanese: なるほどくんは、真宵さまのため なら、いつも火の中、水の中です。
English: You'd walk over miles of hot coals for Mystic Maya, wouldn't you Mr. Nick?

Japanese: （“たとえ”火の中、水の中 ‥‥なんだけど。フツーは）
English: (That would be EVERY time we work a case together...)

Japanese: さ。行こ行こ。 はみちゃんもおいで。
English: Well, what are we waiting for? Let's go already! You can come along too, Pearly.

Japanese: はい！　おともします。
English: Goody! I get to come! I get to come!

Japanese: アトラクション・ショーを取材する ためのカメラだろう。
English: I'm going to say these cameras were set up to cover the post- ceremony stage show.

Japanese: カメラほったらかして、 どこ行っちゃったのかな。みんな。
English: But I wonder where everyone's gone, abandoning their cameras like this...

Japanese: （やっぱり、何か事件が‥‥？）
English: (I guess something big must have happened...)

Japanese: あ。なんか２階におシャレな レストランがあるらしいよ。
English: Wow, it looks like there's a fancy restaurant on the second floor!

Japanese: まだ食べる気なの？
English: You STILL feel like eating?

Japanese: せっかくだしね。
English: I mean, since we're here, you know?

Japanese: （何がせっかくなんだ‥‥）
English: (Don't pull the "since we're here" trick on me...)

Japanese: それにしても、リッパな お屋敷ですね。
English: What a beautiful mansion.

Japanese: なるほどくんは、こんな 世界で生活しているのですか。
English: Do you live in this wonderful world every day, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: いやいや。ぼくも初めてだよ、 こんなところ。
English: Oh, no, this is my first time in a place like this.

Japanese: アトラクション・ショーの ステージだ。
English: It's the stage for the post- ceremony stage show.

Japanese: ショーが終わったら、記者会見も あるハズだったんですが。
English: There was supposed to be a press meeting after the show, but now...

Japanese: “トノサマンのコクハク”か。 聞けなくなっちゃいましたね。
English: It looks like no one will hear what the Nickel Samurai had to confess after all.

Japanese: せっかく来たんだし、みんなで イスにすわって写真撮ろうよ。
English: We're here, so we might as well take some pictures. OK, everyone, sit in a chair!

Japanese: ‥‥でも、もうないぞ。 フィルム。
English: But we're all out of film...

Japanese: カメラならそこらへんに 立ってるでしょ。いっぱい。
English: Don't worry about it. There's a zillion cameras over there we can borrow.

Japanese: コラコラ。勝手に いじるなって。
English: Hey, hey! You can't just go and "borrow" an expensive professional camera like that!

Japanese: オバチャン、 どうしてこんなところに？
English: Ms. Oldbag, what are you doing here?

Japanese: 英都撮影所のほうは‥‥？
English: What happened to your position at Global Studios...?

Japanese: ああ。英都撮影所ネ、あれから さらにヒトが減らされてサ。
English: Oh, that old place? Well, since "that" incident, they've been letting people go.

Japanese: 警備チームからは、オバチャンが ホッポリだされたのョ。
English: When they cut the security team, I got the pink slip.

Japanese: 何かやっちゃったんですか？
English: What incident? What did you do?

Japanese: ナニもしてないョッ！
English: I didn't do anything, you young'un!

Japanese: ‥‥ただ、あったろ？ １年前の、あの事件。
English: Don't you remember? That incident a year ago...

Japanese: オバチャンがサッソウと 証言台に立ってサ。
English: When this lady got on the witness stand and testified.

Japanese: ああ‥‥。
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: アンタ、イジめてくれたじゃない？ この、か弱いオバチャンのコト。
English: And you! Weren't you the one who was bullying me; this fragile girl at heart!?

Japanese: え。
English: Um... I plead the fifth.

Japanese: どうやらアレがネ。上の連中の 気にさわったみたいでサ。
English: But you know, I think maybe I rubbed the upper management the wrong way by testifying.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだョ！ ゼーンブ、アンタのせいだョ！
English: Yes, that has to be it! Everything is all your fault!

Japanese: ぼぼ、ぼくですか？
English: M-Me!?

Japanese: 最初は、ぼでーがーどでも やろうかとも思ったワケ。
English: I thought about being a bodyguard at first, after being handed ol' pinky.

Japanese: ボディガード？
English: You? A bodyguard?

Japanese: アンタのトモダチのさ。 赤くてヒラヒラのイイオトコ。
English: For your friend! That fiery, good-looking guy with the red jacket and the ruffles.

Japanese: 御剣検事のコトかな？
English: M-Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: でもサ。
English: But...

Japanese: そのようなアレは、困る。
English: That sort of arrangement would be entirely too troublesome for me.

Japanese: ‥‥とか言われちゃってサ。
English: That's what he said to me.

Japanese: オバチャン、セツないよッ！
English: What did I ever do to deserve that!?

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: あの‥‥。 何があったんですか？
English: Um... So, did something happen?

Japanese: くわしいコトは知らないケド。
English: I don't have all the details,

Japanese: ‥‥でもサ。なんか、 また起こったみたいだョ。事件。
English: but, it looks like another one of those "incidents" happened again.

Japanese: じ、事件‥‥ですか？
English: A-An "incident"? Like a "murder" kind of "incident"?

Japanese: あー。オバチャン、 魔性のオンナだからねェ。
English: Maybe. You see, I'm a bit of a devilish woman.

Japanese: オバチャンの行くところ、 血の雨がドシャ降るワケよ。
English: So wherever I go, a rain of blood will come pouring down. Bet you didn't know that!

Japanese: ‥‥警備員、やめたほうが いいんじゃないですか？
English: Um, then shouldn't you quit being a security guard, at least for other people's sake?

Japanese: ウッサイ子だネ！
English: Silence, whippersnapper!

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: オバチャン、 どうしてこんなところに？
English: Ms. Oldbag, what are you doing here?

Japanese: 英都撮影所のほうは‥‥？
English: What happened to your position at Global Studios...?

Japanese: ああ。英都撮影所ネ、あれから さらにヒトが減らされてサ。
English: Oh, that old place? Well, since "that" incident, they've been letting people go.

Japanese: 警備チームからは、オバチャンが ホッポリだされたのョ。
English: When they cut the security team, I got the pink slip.

Japanese: それはやはり、あれでしょうか。 “ふしょうじ”とかいう‥‥
English: Um, I guess you must have done something... um... "deh-plor-ible"...

Japanese: ナニもしてないョッ！
English: "Deplorable!?" I would never do such a thing!

Japanese: ‥‥ただ、あったろ？ １年前の、あの事件。
English: Don't you remember? That incident a year ago...

Japanese: オバチャンがサッソウと 証言台に立ってサ。
English: When this lady got on the witness stand and testified.

Japanese: ああ‥‥。
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: アンタ、イジめてくれたじゃない？ この、か弱いオバチャンのコト。
English: And you! Weren't you the one who was bullying me; this fragile girl at heart!?

Japanese: え。
English: Um... I plead the fifth.

Japanese: どうやらアレがネ。上の連中の 気にさわったみたいでサ。
English: But you know, I think maybe I rubbed the upper management the wrong way by testifying.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだョ！ ゼーンブ、アンタのせいだョ！
English: Yes, that has to be it! Everything is all your fault!

Japanese: ぼぼ、ぼくですか？
English: M-Me!?

Japanese: 最初は、ぼでーがーどでも やろうかとも思ったワケ。
English: I thought about being a bodyguard at first, after being handed ol' pinky.

Japanese: ぼでーがーど？
English: "Body...guard?"

Japanese: アンタのトモダチのさ。 赤くてヒラヒラのイイオトコ。
English: For your friend! That fiery, good-looking guy with the red jacket and the ruffles.

Japanese: （‥‥御剣のことか‥‥？）
English: (She's not talking about Edgeworth, is she...?)

Japanese: でもサ。
English: But...

Japanese: そのようなアレは、困る。
English: That sort of arrangement would be entirely too troublesome for me.

Japanese: ‥‥とか言われちゃってサ。
English: That's what he said to me.

Japanese: オバチャン、セツないよッ！
English: What did I ever do to deserve that!?

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。 いったい、何があったんですか？
English: I'm sorry, but what in the world just happened?

Japanese: くわしいコトは知らないケド。
English: I don't have all the details,

Japanese: ‥‥でもサ。なんか、 また起こったみたいだョ。事件。
English: but, it looks like another one of those "incidents" happened again.

Japanese: じ、事件‥‥ですか？
English: A-An "incident"? Like a "murder" kind of "incident"?

Japanese: あー。オバチャン、 魔性のオンナだからねェ。
English: Maybe. You see, I'm a bit of a devilish woman.

Japanese: オバチャンの行くところ、 血の雨がドシャ降るワケよ。
English: So wherever I go, a rain of blood will come pouring down. Bet you didn't know that!

Japanese: ‥‥警備員、やめたほうが いいんじゃないですか？
English: Um, then shouldn't you quit being a security guard, at least for other people's sake?

Japanese: ウッサイ子だネ！
English: Silence, whippersnapper!

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: 今、オバチャンの目は プロになってるんだョ！
English: These eyes of mine are real pros now!

Japanese: “ぷろ”‥‥？ なんのプロですか？
English: "Pros"? Pros at doing what?

Japanese: ケービに決まってんでショ！ ケービケービ！
English: At doing police work, of course! My eyes are like a hawk!

Japanese: 今、ジケンが起こってんのョ！ ジケンジケン！
English: And my professional eyes spot a messy incident! A messy, bloody incident!

Japanese: だから、そんなちまちました モン、見てるヒマないんだョ！
English: That's why I don't have time for you meddling kids and your petty little things!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ホテル・バンドー　ロビー
English: March 20 Gatewater Hotel Hotel Lobby

Japanese: ‥‥あ！ 成歩堂さん、どうでした？
English: Hey! So what's going on, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: どうも、藤見野　イサオさんが 亡くなったみたいです。
English: It looks like Juan Corrida has been killed.

Japanese: ななな、なんですって！ イサオくんが！
English: Wh-Wh-What!? Juan is... He's...!?

Japanese: 殺害されたそうです。 容疑者も逮捕されました。
English: It looks like he was murdered... And a suspect was arrested.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟さんが、ね。
English: That suspect is Mr. Matt Engarde.

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥！
English: Y-You're joking, right!?

Japanese: 忍者ナンジャ殺害容疑で、 トノサマンが逮捕されたわけです。
English: Nope. They arrested the Nickel Samurai on suspicion of murdering the Jammin' Ninja.

Japanese: うう‥‥またですか‥‥
English: Ooogh... Not again... I feel sick.
"Again"...?

Japanese: ‥‥１年ぐらい前にね。 似たような事件があったんだよ。
English: ...About a year ago, something just like this happened, Pearls.

Japanese: そんな、まさか‥‥ オートロくんが！
English: I still can't believe... No way... No way Matt would...

Japanese: （‥‥？　荷星さん、 手に何を持ってるんだ‥‥？）
English: What's Mr. Powers got in his hand...?)

Japanese: ‥‥あ。そうだ。 コレ‥‥成歩堂さんに。
English: ...Oh, before I forget... This... This is for you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: さっき、ボーイのかたから あずかったんですけど。
English: I got this from the bellboy that came by earlier.

Japanese: 《トランシーバー》を 荷星さんから受けとった。
English: Radio Transceiver received from Mr. Powers.

Japanese: ‥‥これを、ぼくに‥‥？
English: ...For me...? But why?

Japanese: ええ。弁護士の 成歩堂　龍一さんに、って。
English: I don't know. All he said was it was for Mr. Phoenix Wright, the attorney.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば‥‥ おそいですね。真宵さま。
English: Um... I was just wondering... Where's Mystic Maya? She's been gone a long time.

Japanese: （言われてみれば‥‥ 電話に呼ばれたっきりだな）
English: (Now that she mentions it, she was only going to answer a phone call...)

Japanese: まいごになってるのかも しれません。
English: Maybe she got lost.

Japanese: わたくし、ちょっと見てきます。
English: I'll take a quick look around for her.

Japanese: ‥‥ピピピピピピ‥‥
English: ...*beep beep beep*...

Japanese: わっ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ど、どうしました、 なるほどくん！
English: Wh-What is it, Mr. Nick!?

Japanese: え？　ぼ、ぼく‥‥？
English: Huh? I... I'm not sure...

Japanese: （このトランシーバーが‥‥？）
English: (Did this transceiver just...?)

Japanese: は、はい。成歩堂ですけど‥‥
English: Y-Yes, hello? Wright here...

Japanese: 弁護士の成歩堂　龍一さま、 でございますか？
English: Is this Mr. Phoenix Wright, the attorney?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥？
English: And you are...!?

Japanese: 私のことなど、 どうでもいいではないですか。
English: You don't need to know who I am.

Japanese: それよりも、問題なのは‥‥
English: I think you have other things to be concerning yourself with... Such as...

Japanese: たすけてッ！　なるほどくん！
English: Heeelp! Niiiiick!

Japanese: ま‥‥真宵さま！
English: M-Mystic Maya!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん‥‥？
English: Maya...?

Japanese: 問題なのは、お連れサマのイノチ。 ‥‥ちがいますか？
English: So, Mr. Wright. Wouldn't you agree that the more important issue is the fate of the girl?

Japanese: （イノチ‥‥だって‥‥？）
English: (Her fate? Does he mean what I think he means...?)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！　どこにいるんだ！ ‥‥ブジなんだよな！
English: Maya! Where are you!? Are you hurt!?

Japanese: まあまあ‥‥。 そう、コーフンなさらずに。
English: Come now. Don't fall apart on me yet.

Japanese: （こ‥‥これは‥‥まさか！）
English: (Th-This... No! This can't be!)

Japanese: さて。弁護士さんに お聞きしましょう。
English: Now that I have your attention, Mr. Attorney,

Japanese: 私はこれから、あなたに ある要求をいたします。
English: I have a modest proposal for you.

Japanese: それと引きかえに、この“ニモツ” をお返しする。
English: If you do what I require, then I will return to you your valuable "item" unharmed.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたがたのコトバでは、 これをなんと呼びますか？
English: ...What is this called again in your fancy lawyer terms?

Japanese: ‥‥“営利誘拐”‥‥
English: ..."Kidnapping for ransom"...

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Yes, that's it.

Japanese: ‥‥これは、誘拐なのですよ。
English: This is a kidnapping.

Japanese: きゃああああああああああああっ！ 真宵さま！　真宵さまああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah! Mystic Maya! Mystic Mayaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!!

Japanese: （目の前が‥‥真っ暗になった）
English: (M-My sight... Everything's fading away...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんが‥‥ 真宵ちゃんが‥‥誘拐！）
English: (Maya... Maya... Maya's been kidnapped!)

Japanese: ‥‥ますか？ 成歩堂さん。聞こえますか？
English: You there? Mr. Wright, are you there?

Japanese: い‥‥いくらだ！ いくらほしいんだッ！
English: H-How much!? How much do you want!?

Japanese: さすが、弁護士さんですね。 ものわかりがよろしい。
English: Very good, Mr. Attorney. I'm glad you have such a good grasp of the situation.

Japanese: 早く要求を言え！ そして、彼女を‥‥
English: Hurry up and state your condition! And then return Maya --

Japanese: 私の要求は、 カネではありません。
English: Money is not what I seek.
...What?

Japanese: 要求は‥‥判決です。 無罪判決を、ひとつ。
English: What I want is a certain verdict. I would like a complete acquittal.

Japanese: 無罪判決だと！ いったいおまえ、何を‥‥？
English: Complete acquittal!? Wh-What in the world have you done to need...!?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたが弁護するのは、 私ではありません。
English: I am not the person you will be representing.

Japanese: なんだと‥‥
English: What...?

Japanese: あなたは今、ホテル・バンドーに いらっしゃる。
English: You are currently at the Gatewater Hotel, are you not?

Japanese: そこではたった今、殺人事件が 起こったはずです。
English: And I know that a murder has just taken place there.

Japanese: ‥‥藤見野　イサオが殺害された。 容疑者として、王都楼　真悟が‥‥
English: Juan Corrida was killed, and the suspect is Matt Engarde...

Japanese: さすが、ヤリ手の弁護士さんだ。
English: You are, as expected, quite on top of things, Mr. Attorney.

Japanese: ‥‥話はカンタンです。
English: Now then. What I want is very simple.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟を 無罪にしていただきたい。
English: I want you to obtain a complete acquittal for Matt Engarde.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟を‥‥？
English: M-Matt Engarde? But why?

Japanese: 彼は、だれも殺していません。 それは私が保証します。ただ‥‥
English: He did not kill anyone. I can attest to that. However...

Japanese: ただ‥‥なんだ！
English: H-However!? However what!?

Japanese: だれかが、彼をツミに 落とそうとしています。
English: However, someone is framing him for the murder.

Japanese: 殺人のツミを着せようとしている。 ‥‥アタマのいい、だれかが‥‥
English: A very smart someone who is setting him up to take the fall...

Japanese: （そんなコト、“ハイそうですか” なんて、信じられるか！）
English: (If I agree and do what he wants, can I believe he'll keep his end of the bargain!?)

Japanese: ‥‥私を信じないのは、 あなたの自由です。
English: You are, of course, at liberty to believe me or not.

Japanese: しかし、これだけは 信じざるを得ない。
English: However, there is one thing you can take as fact.

Japanese: あなたの大切な“ニモツ”は 今、私のところにあるのです。
English: Right now, your very precious "item" is with me, in my possession.

Japanese: うっ‥‥うう。たすけてよお‥‥
English: Mmngnhhh... Heeelp!... Nngmph... NIIIICK!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん‥‥！
English: Maya!

Japanese: タイム・リミットは２日。
English: You have two days.

Japanese: おそらく今夜。そちらでは 事情聴取がおこなわれる。
English: Of course, tonight, he will be in questioning with the police.

Japanese: 審理は２日後になるでしょう。
English: But the trial is in two days.

Japanese: その法廷で、無罪判決を 勝ちとっていただきたい。
English: At that trial, you will win a not guilty verdict.

Japanese: チャンスは、１回きりです。
English: Remember, you only have one chance. One chance, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: １回‥‥！ たった１回で、無罪判決だと！
English: O-One...!? Y-You expect me to get a not guilty in one trial day!?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。あなたに 選択の余地は、ないのです。
English: Yes, exactly. I don't believe I was wrong in choosing you, so don't let me down.

Japanese: う‥‥ッ！
English: Aagh!

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、そうそう。 せっかく誘拐をしたのですから、
English: Oh, yes, that's right. Now that I am playing the role of the kidnapper,

Japanese: やはり、このセリフは 言っておきませんとね。
English: I can't pass up this chance to say,

Japanese: “ケイサツには知らせるな” ‥‥まあ。念のため、ですけれど。
English: "And don't even think about callin' the cops!" ...Hmm, not great, but you get the idea.

Japanese: く、くそっ！ いったい‥‥
English: D-Dammit! Who...

Japanese: いったい、 お前はだれなんだ！
English: Who the hell are you!?

Japanese: ‥‥いいでしょう。 教えてあげますよ。
English: Alright, I'll tell you that much.

Japanese: 私の名前は‥‥
English: My name is...

Japanese: ‥‥コロシヤ。
English: ...De Killer.

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！ まよい‥‥真宵さまは！
English: M-Mr. Nick! Wh-Where is Mystic Maya!?

Japanese: ‥‥誘拐された。
English: ...She's been kidnapped.

Japanese: わ‥‥わたくしのせい ‥‥わたくしのせいです！
English: N-No... It's all my fault!

Japanese: あのとき、真宵さまと いっしょに行っていれば‥‥！
English: If only I had gone with Mystic Maya...!

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃんの せいじゃないよ。
English: It's not your fault, Pearls.

Japanese: わたくしのせいです！ 真宵さまああ！　ええええええん！
English: But it is! It is! Mystic Mayaaaaaaaaaa! Waaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん‥‥やっぱり、 警察に知らせたほうが‥‥！
English: Mr. Wright... I think we... I think we should tell the police what it's going on...!

Japanese: （ダメだ！　知らせたら、 真宵ちゃんのイノチは‥‥）
English: (No! We can't do that! If we do, who knows what will happen to Maya...)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ さっきの刑事さんなら‥‥？
English: Mr. Nick! What about the detective we saw earlier...?

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコ刑事‥‥！）
English: (...Detective Gumshoe!)

Japanese: そうだね！ ちょっと、行ってくる！
English: Yes, that's it! Wait here, and I'll be right back!

Japanese: 春美さんのことは まかせてください！
English: Alright! I'll take care of Pearl while you're away!

Japanese: なんスとおおおおおおおおおッ！ ユーカイッスうううううううッ！
English: WHAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAT!? RAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAANSOM!?

Japanese: こ、声が高いですよ！
English: Shhh! Not so loud!

Japanese: ‥‥要求は、王都楼　真悟の 無罪判決です。
English: ...The ransom is complete acquittal for Matt Engarde.

Japanese: 交換条件が“無罪判決”‥‥？
English: Wait, the deal is "complete acquittal" in exchange for Maya, you mean...?

Japanese: ということはモチロン、 王都楼は真犯人というコトッスな！
English: Then this means Matt Engarde is obviously the killer, pal! No doubt about it!

Japanese: でも、そいつが言うには、 王都楼さんは無実だ、って‥‥
English: But the guy said that Mr. Engarde is innocent...

Japanese: 誘拐犯の言うコトなんて、 信じちゃダメッス！
English: You really believe what a KIDNAPPER tells you, pal!?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ホントに無実なら、 誘拐なんてする必要がない）
English: (I guess he has a point... If Engarde is really innocent, then why the kidnapping?)

Japanese: それに、ジッサイ、彼に対する 不利な証拠がザクザク出てるッス。
English: And on top of everything else, there's all this evidence we keep finding.
Huh?

Japanese: 鑑識の連中がカンパイしてたッス。 “今夜は大漁だ”って。
English: The guys with the eagle eyes are really lapping it up like they just caught a huge fish.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Umm...

Japanese: ‥‥あんまり証拠がありすぎて、 逆にアヤシイ気がするッス。
English: But it's strange. There's so much evidence that it feels like something's wrong.

Japanese: 証拠が‥‥ありすぎる？
English: There's too much evidence? Is that possible?

Japanese: （そういえば、言ってたな。 “ツミを着せようとしている”と）
English: (Actually, didn't the kidnapper say something about Engarde being "set-up"...?)

Japanese: とにかく、今夜はこの会場から 出すことはできないッス。
English: Anyway, looks like you won't be leaving here tonight, pal.

Japanese: おとなしく取り調べを受けて、 明日。行動を開始するッス。
English: Just sit tight and cooperate with the investigation. Tomorrow you can start yours.

Japanese: ‥‥わ、わかりました‥‥。 （やむを得ないのか‥‥）
English: ...A-Alright... (Isn't there another way...?)

Japanese: 犯人をシゲキすることだけは しちゃダメッス。
English: We've also got to be careful about pushing the kidnapper the wrong way.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: You're right...
Date: ???  Time: ??? Location: ???

Japanese: うー、アタマいたい‥‥
English: Nnngh... Oww, my head...

Japanese: （こ‥‥ここ、どこ！ あのホテル、なのかな‥‥）
English: (Wh-Where... Where am I!? I wonder if I'm still in that hotel...)

Japanese: （あ、あたし ‥‥どうなっちゃったの‥‥？）
English: (Wh-What happened to me...?)

Japanese: なるほどくーん‥‥ はみちゃあん！
English: Nick... Pearly!

Japanese: （や、やめてよ‥‥冗談は‥‥）
English: (Come on, you guys. This isn't funny anymore...)

Japanese: ‥‥ガチャガチャ‥‥
English: ...*ka-click*...

Japanese: 目がさめましたか。
English: I see you have awoken.

Japanese: きゃあああああっ！ だだだだ、ダレですかッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaaah! Wh-Wh-Wh-Who are you!?

Japanese: 私ですか？ ‥‥私は、コロシヤです。
English: Me? ...I am known as "De Killer".

Japanese: こ‥‥こ‥‥こ‥‥ころしや‥‥ （あたし‥‥殺されちゃうの！）
English: "D-De Killer"!? You mean like an "ASSASSIN"!? (I... I'm too young to die!)

Japanese: ‥‥ご安心なさい。
English: Don't worry.

Japanese: 私のエモノは、あなたではない。 ‥‥今は、まだ。
English: You are not my target. ...For now anyway.

Japanese: う、うわああああああああああん！ なるほどくん！　なるほどくうん！
English: A-Aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah! Nick! Nick! Where are you!?

Japanese: ‥‥たすけてよぉぉ‥‥
English: ...Help me...

Japanese: そう。あなたを助けられるのは ‥‥成歩堂　龍一だけ、でしょう。
English: Yes, that's right. Only one person can save you now, and that is Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥え‥‥な。な、 なるほどくんが‥‥？
English: ...Huh? Wh-What? Nick... Nick's going to save me...?

Japanese: おとなしくしていなさい。 これは、ビジネスなのです。
English: Calm down and be a good girl. Think of this as a business transaction.

Japanese: び‥‥びじねす？
English: A... A business transaction?

Japanese: これから、彼に連絡をとります。
English: I am going to contact him now.

Japanese: あなたも、出演を よろしくおねがいします。
English: I hope you'll cooperate and play your assigned role well.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん‥‥
English: ...Nick...

Japanese: あたし、どうなっちゃったの‥‥ なるほどくん‥‥　はみちゃん‥‥
English: What's going to happen to me? Nick... Pearls...

Japanese: ‥‥おねえちゃあん‥‥
弁護士の成歩堂　龍一さま、 でございますか？
English: Is this Mr. Phoenix Wright, the attorney?

Japanese: ‥‥私のことなど、 どうでもいいではないですか。
English: You don't need to know who I am.

Japanese: それよりも、問題なのは‥‥
English: I think you have other things to be concerning yourself with... Such as...

Japanese: 取材に来た人たちの カメラだろう。
English: These cameras must belong to the press that came to cover the conference.

Japanese: どれも本格的なシロモノで、 ステージをねらっている。
English: They're all basically doing the same thing: staring at the empty stage.

Japanese: 吹き抜けのロビーは、 とにかく大きな空間に感じる。
English: A lobby with a grand staircase always feels enormous.

Japanese: そして今、この空間には、 事件の緊張感がみなぎっている。
English: But right now, the only thing filling this enormous room is anxiety about the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥これ以上、悪いことが 起こらなければいいけど‥‥。
English: ...I really hope nothing else bad happens here...

Japanese: アトラクション・ショーと 記者会見のために用意されたイス。
English: Seats for the spectators of the post-ceremony show and the press conference.

Japanese: ‥‥両方とも、 ムダになってしまった。
English: But it's too bad neither event is going to be held now.

Japanese: オートロくんは、今、最も イキオイがある俳優です。
English: Matt is the most energetic and active actor out there right now.

Japanese: 今回のトノサマン役が、 彼の人気を決定づけたのですが‥‥
English: The Nickel Samurai really sealed his place as a pop idol, but...

Japanese: これがまた、イサオくんの ライバル意識に火をつけちゃって。
English: he kept adding fuel to the fire of his rivalry with Juan anyway.

Japanese: イサオさん‥‥ 忍者ナンジャのかた、ですね。
English: Mr. Juan... He's the Jammin' Ninja, right?

Japanese: あの２人、何かと ぶつかるワリには‥‥
English: Those two would butt heads over everything they could think of.

Japanese: わずかな差で、かならず オートロくんが勝っちゃうんです。
English: But I'd say that Matt was the one who almost always came out on top.

Japanese: （あるよな‥‥そういう カンケイ、って）
English: (I guess some people only know how to relate to others by butting heads like a ram...)

Japanese: イサオくん、“今度こそは”って、 対抗番組をぶつけたんですよ。
English: Juan said that he'd take Matt on this time too, so he joined a rival TV show.

Japanese: それが、《忍者ナンジャ》‥‥
English: And that was "The Jammin' Ninja"...?

Japanese: スマートなトノサマンと、 アツくるしい忍者ナンジャ‥‥
English: The stylish Nickel Samurai and the burning Jammin' Ninja...

Japanese: とにかく、ふたりの争いは 番組をこえて、ドロヌマでしたね。
English: Well, things turned messy real fast with those two using their shows for their "war".

Japanese: ‥‥その決着が 今夜、ついたワケです。
English: And the final tally... Look around... It's pretty obvious how things ended up.

Japanese: （グランプリ‥‥また、 トノサマンが勝ったわけか‥‥）
English: (Matt even won the Grand Prix this year... The final win over his rival, I guess...)

Japanese: 王都楼さん、記者会見を するつもりだったんですよね？
English: Mr. Engarde was going to hold a press conference, wasn't he?

Japanese: そう聞いていますよ。
English: That's what I heard.

Japanese: まあ、正確には“トノサマン”の 記者会見ですけど。
English: Though if you wanted to get technical about it, it was the Nickel Samurai's conference.

Japanese: トノサマンの‥‥？
English: The Nickel Samurai's...?

Japanese: ええ。着ぐるみに入ったまま、 会見をするはずだったんです。
English: Yeah. He was supposed to wear his costume and give the press conference that way.

Japanese: 着ぐるみに‥‥？ なぜ、そんなことを？
English: So he was supposed to be in- costume...? But why?

Japanese: さあ‥‥ ボクには、なんとも。
English: I'm not sure... They don't keep me in the loop anymore.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんたちが ここから出てすぐでしたね。
English: It was almost right after you guys left.

Japanese: おじいさんのボーイさんが あらわれまして。
English: An old man who's a bellboy at this hotel showed up.

Japanese: ぼくに、これを、と。
English: And that's when he told you to give this to me...?

Japanese: そうです。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: （なんで トランシーバーなんか‥‥）
English: (Why... Why give me this transceiver...?)

Japanese: あれ。そういえば いませんね、真宵さん。
English: Hey, where did Maya go off to?

Japanese: なんか、電話に 呼び出されまして。
English: She got a phone call.

Japanese: ああ、そういえばさっき、 フロントのほうへ。
English: Oh yeah, that's right. She was heading for the front desk just now.

Japanese: ミョーに 目立ちますからね、彼女。
English: She sure stands out in a crowd, doesn't she?

Japanese: そんなにコワいんですかねえ、 このカオ。
English: Hey, is my face really all that scary?

Japanese: 子どもたちはだれも 近よって来ないし‥‥
English: Kids won't come anywhere near me...

Japanese: ボクがアタマをかくと、 みなさんビクッとするし‥‥
English: But when I cover my face, then they're all shocked because they figure out who I am...

Japanese: わたくしは、とても やさしいおカオだと思います。
English: I think your face makes you look like a very kind person!

Japanese: （荷星さん、 ナミダぐんじゃったぞ‥‥）
English: (Aww... Mr. Powers is getting teary eyed.)

Japanese: オートロくん、ボクの 後輩なんですけどね。
English: Matt's younger than me,

Japanese: やっぱり、出てますよ。 スターのオーラが。
English: but you can practically see his star potential.

Japanese: すたーのおおら‥‥ですか。
English: His star po-ten-shial...?

Japanese: サインもらっちゃいましたもん、 ボク。
English: I got his autograph the other day.

Japanese: ‥‥それはちょっと、 カッコわるいですね。
English: ...Feels kind of wrong now, doesn't it?

Japanese: オートロくんがイサオくんを‥‥ そんなコト、ボクは信じません！
English: I don't care what people say! Matt didn't kill Juan! I know he didn't!

Japanese: デビューが近かったんですよ。 オートロくんと。
English: He debuted around the same time as Matt in everything, you know.

Japanese: そうなのですか。
English: Really?

Japanese: “スター・のどじまん大会”やら “スター・水泳大会”やら‥‥
English: Yeah, it started out small... First it was singing contests, then swimming competitions...

Japanese: “スター・ボウリング大会”やら “スター・大忘年会”やら‥‥
English: Then it was bowling tournaments, and then it was who could throw the best

Japanese: ナニかと張り合っていましたねえ。
English: New Year's parties... Juan was always trying to one-up Matt.

Japanese: 最近は、だんだん エスカレートしてきて‥‥
English: But lately, those two were escalating to more and more dangerous things...

Japanese: タイヘンなコトになるんじゃないか って、シンパイしてたんですよ。
English: I thought that no good would come of it all, so I began to worry.

Japanese: （そしてついに‥‥殺人事件が 起こってしまったワケか）
English: (Too bad Juan's story ended so soon...)

Japanese: ３月２１日　午前　８時１１分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: March 21, 8:11 AM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: おはようございますッ！
English: Good morning!

Japanese: ああ、おはよう。
English: Ah, morning Pearls.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあさっそく！ その、王都楼さんに面会を‥‥
English: Mr. Nick! Mr. Nick! Come on! Let's hurry and go see Mr. Engarde!

Japanese: もう少し待とう。 ‥‥面会は９時からだから。
English: We have to wait a bit, Pearls. Visiting hours don't start until 9 AM, so...

Japanese: そうなのですか？
English: Oh, I see.

Japanese: ああ、真宵さま ‥‥わたくしがあのとき‥‥
English: Ah, Mystic Maya! If only... If only I went with you that time...

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、ゆうべから ずっとこんな調子だ）
English: (Poor Pearls... She's been like this since last night...)

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事が手をまわして くれたおかげで‥‥）
English: (We managed to get home somehow, yesterday evening...)

Japanese: （なんとかゆうべは 帰ってこられたけど‥‥）
English: (Thanks to Detective Gumshoe gently holding her by the hand and leading her here...)

Japanese: （このようすじゃ春美ちゃん、 ゼンゼン眠ってないみたいだ）
English: (But by the looks of it, I don't think Pearls got any sleep at all last night.)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ブジですよね‥‥真宵さまは！
English: Mr. Nick! Mystic Maya... She's alright, right!?

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。 かならずぼくが、助けるから。
English: Yeah, she's alright. Either way, I'm going to save her. You can trust me on that.

Japanese: おねがいします。 ‥‥おねがいします‥‥
English: Please... Please... Help her...

Japanese: （春美ちゃんがこうして とりみだしているおかげで‥‥）
English: (I'm only able to stay this calm and collected...)

Japanese: （ぼくのほうは、なんとか 冷静をたもつことができている）
English: (Because Pearls is doing the crying for the both of us...)

Japanese: ‥‥見慣れた事務所の どこに視線を投げても‥‥
English: ...No matter where I look in this familiar space I call my office...

Japanese: 気をまぎらわすことが できない。
English: No, I can't let my mind wander now!

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、時間がない。 行動しなくちゃ‥‥！
English: I don't have the time! I've got to get going on this case!

Japanese: あの‥‥ひとつ、 気になることがあるんです。
English: Um... I have a thought...
Huh?

Japanese: 王都楼という殿方の弁護 ‥‥なさるんですよね。
English: You're going to represent Mr. Engarde... aren't you?

Japanese: うん。 なんとしてもね。
English: Yeah. I don't really seem to have a choice.

Japanese: もし、そのかたが‥‥ 本当にその‥‥真犯人だったら。
English: Um, but what if... What if he... ...is the real murderer?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくんは、どうなさる おつもりなのですか？
English: What would you do then, Mr. Nick...?

Japanese: 真宵さまを助けるために、 サツジンハンを、ムザイに‥‥
English: Would you fight to get a not guilty for a murderer to save Mystic Maya...?

Japanese: 春美ちゃん。
English: Pearls...

Japanese: まずは、会ってみようよ。 王都楼さんに。
English: Let's talk to Mr. Engarde first, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥悪い方向にばっかり 考えても、しかたないから。
English: ...We can think all the bad things we want, but it doesn't change a thing.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですよね‥‥。
English: Y-Yeah, you're right...

Japanese: すみません。わたくし‥‥ 自分でも、止められなくて。
English: I'm sorry. I... I can't stop thinking about it...

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥真宵ちゃんのこと、 本当に心配してくれてるんだね。
English: Pearls... You're really worried about Maya, aren't you?

Japanese: だって。わたくしにはもう、 他にはだれもいませんから。
English: I... I don't have anyone else left in this world.

Japanese: だれも‥‥？
English: What do you mean...?

Japanese: 家族です。
English: My family's all gone...
(...Her family...)

Japanese: おとうさまは‥‥
English: My father...

Japanese: おかあさまをおいて、 里を出て行ってしまいました。
English: He left my mother and the village behind and went away.

Japanese: そうなんだ‥‥。
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: そして‥‥おかあさまも。
English: And... my mother...

Japanese: わたくしのために、 あんなことに‥‥
English: She did that thing... all for me...

Japanese: 真宵さまは、わたくしのこと、 ほんとうの妹のように‥‥
English: Mystic Maya... She's like a sister to me...

Japanese: わたくしには、 真宵さましか‥‥いないの。
English: She is all I have left in this world...

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥？
English: Pearls...?

Japanese: （ウワの空、か。 今は、ムリないな‥‥）
English: (She looks out of it... Maybe I should leave her alone...)

Japanese: 同日　午前　８時５７分 留置所　面会室
English: March 21, 8:57 AM Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: （面会が始まる時間を待ちきれず、 ぼくたちは留置所にやってきた）
English: (We couldn't wait for visiting hours to start, so Pearls and I came down here early...)

Japanese: （忍者ナンジャ殺害の容疑を かけられた、トノサマンに‥‥）
English: (To visit one Nickel Samurai, charged with the murder of the Jammin' Ninja...)

Japanese: ど、どうも ‥‥おはようございます。
English: G-Good morning. How are you today?

Japanese: このたびは、とんだことで‥‥
English: I know this situation might be a little tough for you...

Japanese: あの。ぼくたちは‥‥
English: Um... We're...

Japanese: あ。すみません‥‥。
English: Oh, sorry dude...

Japanese: ボク、もう、入ってるし‥‥。
English: I already signed up...

Japanese: は、はい‥‥？
English: E-Excuse me...?

Japanese: 生命保険。‥‥ボクのシゴト、 キケンはつきものだから‥‥。
English: I already have life insurance. I signed up a long time ago cause my job is, you know...

Japanese: いやいやいや。ぼく、 保険の勧誘員じゃありませんよ！
English: Oh! No no no... We're not insurance salespeople!

Japanese: あ。そうなんですか‥‥。
English: Really...?

Japanese: でも、ボク、今は いりませんから。
English: Dude, I really don't need that right now either.

Japanese: 消火器。だいいちここ、 ボクのウチじゃないし‥‥。
English: Fire extinguishers. I mean, this building isn't my house, so...

Japanese: いやいやいや。消火器の 訪問販売でもないですよ！
English: No no no... We're not here to sell you fire extinguishers either!

Japanese: ぼくは、弁護士です。 弁護士の、成歩堂　龍一。
English: I'm a lawyer. My name is Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: べんごし‥‥？
English: A lawyer...?

Japanese: ちょっと待って。マネージャーさん に聞いてみるから。
English: Hold on a sec. I'm gonna ask my manager, OK?

Japanese: なんか、フシギなかたですね。 ‥‥トノサマンさん。
English: The Nickel Samurai sure is a strange person, isn't he?

Japanese: （フシギすぎて ワケがわかんないぞ‥‥）
English: (I think "strange" is an understatement.)

Japanese: あ。すみません‥‥。
English: Sorry about that.

Japanese: やっぱり、 間に合ってますから。
English: You're just in time.

Japanese: え！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: ベンゴシ‥‥ですよね？
English: You're a lawyer dude, right?

Japanese: 今、マネージャーさんが ウデキキを探してくれてるから。
English: My manager's looking for a good one right now, so how about it?

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！
English: M-Mr. Nick! This is our chance!

Japanese: （ここで断られるワケには いかない‥‥！）
English: (I have to make him let me take his case! I have to!)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ。もうそろそろボク、 帰りますから。
English: ...Well, dude, I think it's about time for me to get going.

Japanese: 待ってください！ ‥‥それは困ります！
English: Please, wait! ...I really need to take your case!

Japanese: 依頼人なら他にもいるでしょ？ なんなら紹介しましょうか？
English: There's always other people in need of a lawyer, right? Want me to introduce you to a few?

Japanese: おねがいです！ ‥‥ベンゴ‥‥させてください！
English: Please! Please let Mr. Nick represent you!

Japanese: やれやれ。最近のベンゴシさんは、 客引きにそんな子どもまで使うの？
English: Man, oh man. Lawyers these days. Now you dudes use kids to pull in clients too?

Japanese: あなたのベンゴを引き受けないと、 コロシヤが‥‥ッ！
English: If you don't take me as your lawyer, then De Killer is going to...!

Japanese: ちょっと待って。 ‥‥今、なんて言いました？
English: Wait... What did you just say?

Japanese: “コロシヤ”‥‥ですけど。
English: "De Killer"...

Japanese: こ、コロシヤ‥‥！
English: D-De Killer!?

Japanese: （‥‥どうしたんだ？ 何か考えこんでるぞ‥‥）
English: (...What's he doing? He looks like he's mulling something over...)

Japanese: わかりました。 あなたの言う通りにします。
English: Alright, dude. I accept your terms.

Japanese: は？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ボクのベンゴ‥‥ あなたにおねがいします。
English: I'll let you represent me in court.

Japanese: や‥‥やりました！ なるほどくん！
English: W-We did it! We did it, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥。
English: Uh, yeah...

Japanese: （‥‥なんかスッキリしないぞ。 スナオによろこべない‥‥）
English: (I don't feel any better for it... And he doesn't look too happy either...)

Japanese: なんでも聞いてください。 ボク‥‥協力しますから。
English: Go ahead, ask me anything. I'll help out as much as I can...

Japanese: （‥‥引き出せる情報は まあ、こんなもんかな‥‥）
English: (...I guess this is about all I'm going to get out of him...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Mr. Nick?
Yeah?

Japanese: 最後に１つ、たしかめて おきましょう。
English: Let's ask one last thing.

Japanese: この殿方が、 本当に無実なのかどうか‥‥？
English: Let's test Mr. Engarde to see if he really is innocent or not...

Japanese: そ、そんなこと、 できるわけが‥‥
English: W-We can do that...?

Japanese: できます。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ‥‥これを使えば。
English: If you use this...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんの‥‥勾玉！）
English: (Maya's Magatama!)

Japanese: なるほどくんに、ヒミツを かくすことはできません。
English: He won't be able to hide any secrets from you, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥ゼッタイに。
English: I'm sure of it.
(I get it...!)

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼さん。 ひとつだけ聞きます。
English: ...Mr. Engarde, I'd like to ask you one more question.

Japanese: ‥‥正直に答えてください。
English: Please answer me honestly.

Japanese: なんでしょうか？
English: What is it, dude?

Japanese: あなたは、藤見野　イサオさんを 殺害しましたか？
English: Did you kill Mr. Juan Corrida?

Japanese: 答えてください、王都楼さん‥‥ 電話で相談するのは、ナシです。
English: Please put the phone away and answer this question yourself.

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥ハッキリ答えましょう。
English: Alright. Just so we're clear, dude,

Japanese: ボク、だれも殺してません。 もちろん、藤見野　イサオも。
English: I didn't kill anyone, and that includes Juan Corrida, OK?

Japanese: どうですか？　なるほどくん。
English: Well, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: サイコ・ロックは‥‥現れない。
English: Nothing. Not a chain or lock in sight.

Japanese: ということは‥‥ 信用して、いいと思います。
English: Which means... I think it is alright to trust him.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ）
English: (Yeah, it does appear that way.)

Japanese: （とりあえず依頼人が無実と わかっただけ、少し安心したな）
English: (Well, at least I can breathe a sigh of relief knowing my client is innocent.)

Japanese: あの‥‥。審理は 明日みたいですから‥‥、
English: Um... The trial's tomorrow, right?

Japanese: よろしくおねがいします。
English: I'm counting on you, dude.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 何か言いたげに こちらをジッと見ている。
English: He's so quiet, you could forget he was even there... If he wasn't watching everything.

Japanese: ‥‥うしろにかくしてるのは‥‥ 色紙とサインペンのようだ。
English: He's pushed up against the wall... Kinda like a magnet on a refrigerator.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: オートロさんがたまに チラリと視線を送っている。
English: Mr. Engarde keeps sneaking glances up at the security camera on the wall...

Japanese: 芸能人の悲しいサガ みたいなものなのかな。
English: I wonder if he is still trying to be a star?

Japanese: オートロさん。事件のことを 聞きたいんですけど‥‥
English: Mr. Engarde, I would like to ask you about the murder...

Japanese: あ。それ、取材ですか？
English: Oh, are you covering this for a tabloid as a side job, dude?

Japanese: あの。あの。ボクのコメントは、 事務所を通して‥‥
English: Um, um... Well, if you want my statement on this, you should ask through my staff...

Japanese: いやいやいや。ぼくたちは そういうのじゃなくて‥‥
English: No no no... I'm not asking on behalf of a tabloid...

Japanese: ちょっと待って。社長さんに 聞いてみるから。
English: Hold on a sec. I'm gonna ask the president of the studio, OK?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 だいじょうぶでしょうか‥‥？
English: Is he alright, Mr. Nick...?

Japanese: （正直なトコロ、ダメっぽい‥‥）
English: (If we're talking about Mr. Engarde's brain here, I wouldn't put my money on it.)

Japanese: あ。すみません‥‥。
English: Sorry about that.

Japanese: 『ボクが犯人じゃないことは、 ネオ・フジヤマが知っている！』
English: The studio president said,

Japanese: ‥‥社長さんが そう言っとけ、って。
English: "Even Neo Mt. Fuji itself knows that I'm not the murderer!"

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。なんですか？ ネオ・フジヤマって。
English: Um, Mr. Nick? What's "Neo Mt. Fuji"...?

Japanese: ‥‥トノサマンが活躍する ネオ・エドシティにある山だよ。
English: It's a mountain in the city of Neo Olde Tokyo; the city the Nickel Samurai protects.

Japanese: すみません。あなたのことを 聞かせていただけますか？
English: Sorry to intrude, but I would like to ask you a few personal questions.

Japanese: え。でも、ボク今度、自伝を 出版するから‥‥
English: Um, that's OK, but dude, my autobiography's coming out soon, so...

Japanese: 勝手なコト言っちゃうと、 出版社のヒトが困るし‥‥。
English: If I say stuff without the publisher's approval, then I'm gonna be in real hot water...

Japanese: ちょっと待って。出版社のヒトに 聞いてみるから。
English: Hold on a sec. I'm gonna ask my publisher, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥いいですね。相談できるかたが たくさんいらっしゃって。
English: Mr. Engarde is so lucky! He has so many people he can talk to.

Japanese: （ううう‥‥）
English: (Umm... I don't know if he actually has anyone he can really talk to...)

Japanese: あ。すみません‥‥。
English: Sorry about that.

Japanese: やっぱり、出版社のヒトも “それは困る”って。
English: Like I thought, the publisher said it'd be "real bad" if I said anything, dude.

Japanese: （なんとかならないのか‥‥！）
English: (Does he have a mind of his own!?)

Japanese: きのうのことを 聞かせてもらえますか？
English: Can you tell me about your activities last night?

Japanese: 賞をもらったあとは‥‥ 控え室で休んでいましたよ。
English: After I got the award, I took a break and went back to my room.

Japanese: 受賞記念のアトラクション・ ショーがありましたから。
English: I had that post-ceremony stage show to do.

Japanese: トノサマンの衣装を着たままで。
English: So I was in my Nickel Samurai costume.

Japanese: ずっと、ひとりだったんですか？
English: And you were alone the entire time?

Japanese: マネージャーさんも いそがしそうだったから。
English: My manager was running around being busy, so yeah.

Japanese: ショーのあとに、記者会見も あったはずですけど？
English: Because of the press conference you were supposed to have after the show?

Japanese: だから、知りませんよ。ボク。
English: I told you, dude. I have no idea about any press conference, alright?

Japanese: （おかしいな‥‥。トノサマンが 何か告白する、って‥‥）
English: (That's strange... I thought the Nickel Samurai was going to confess something...)

Japanese: とにかく、控え室を出たら、 なんかさわがしくて‥‥
English: Anyway, when I was leaving my room, that's when I noticed it was kinda noisy...

Japanese: そのときはもう、 イサオさんは‥‥？
English: Mr. Corrida was already dead at that time...?

Japanese: 死んでいたみたいです。 マネージャーさんが言ってました。
English: Yeah, that's what I gathered anyway from my manager.

Japanese: （さっきから、何かといえば “マネージャーさん”だな‥‥）
English: (With the way he's always talking about his manager, it's as if she runs his life.)

Japanese: そこに、コートの刑事が 現れたんですよ。
English: And that's when the detective in the green coat showed up.

Japanese: 身体検査されて‥‥いつのまにか、 タイホされちゃったんです。
English: He searched me... And then, out of the blue, the dude arrested me.

Japanese: おかげさまで。ボクの名前も 知られるようになってきました。
English: Man, I thank my lucky stars that people know my name.

Japanese: 今や、国民的ヒーローですね。
English: Well, you're quite the hero, and you're in the national spotlight.

Japanese: わたくし、 非国民なのでしょうか‥‥
English: I didn't know who he is... Does that mean I'm not a good citizen...?

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、トノサマンが よかったですね。
English: Ah, it's really great to be the Nickel Samurai.

Japanese: 最近は、人気のハバも 広がってきたし。
English: Dude, lately, I just keep on getting more and more popular.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥）
English: (True enough...)

Japanese: （今や《トノサマン・丙！》は 高校生やＯＬにも大人気だ）
English: ("The Nickel Samurai" is very popular among high schoolers and secretaries right now.)

Japanese: （このオートロさん、女性に ミョーに人気あるんだよな‥‥）
English: (I guess Mr. Engarde has a way of catching the eyes of women...)

Japanese: ボクのキャッチフレーズ、 知ってますか？
English: Do you know my motto?

Japanese: “春風のようにサワヤカなアイツ” ‥‥っていうんですけど。
English: "Refreshing like a spring breeze." That's what I am.

Japanese: はるかぜ‥‥
English: A spring breeze...

Japanese: だからね。‥‥こういう スキャンダル、困るんですよ。
English: That's why this kind of scandal is disastrous, dude.

Japanese: 明日にでも、こんなところからは オサラバしないと。
